feature_source.runtime: DirectML on the tracked AMD host

ai_contract.matched_ablation: Append only the sealed causal FinCast point and quantile features to the identical executable CSM model. Reuse the hash-bound Round 51 matrices; do not rerun foundation-model extraction.
ai_contract.numeric_authority: FinCast supplies causal numeric forecast features only. It cannot select a trade, size an order, approve leverage, override risk controls, or receive trading authority.
ai_contract.uplift_rule: AI is retained only if it improves joint proper scores and expected-payoff rank by at least 0.005, and improves paired base and stress economics without worse drawdown, concentration, or temporal stability. A mixed result fails.

chronological_roles.evaluation: 2023-06-09 through 2023-06-14 UTC; already consumed and diagnostic only
chronological_roles.policy_calibration: 2023-06-07 through 2023-06-08 UTC; both UTC days must pass separately
chronological_roles.role_boundary_rule: Every feature, entry, path quote, exit, and target must finish before the next role starts. Side-specific support and exits are purged independently.

claims.reason: The complete interval has been repeatedly inspected. Round 53 is a selection-contaminated mechanism screen and cannot confirm generalization, profitability, or AI uplift.

data_contract.event_source: Official Binance Data Vision USD-M daily archives with checksum-verified warehouse ingestion
data_contract.historical_depth_limit: bookDepth is sampled aggregate percentage-band depth, not full L2 queue evidence. Passive-fill or queue-position claims are prohibited.

economic_screen.stress_rule: Reprice the exact base ledger with the frozen stress protection delay and two additional basis points per side. Never reselect trades under stress.
economic_screen.threshold_selection: For each fixed coverage and symbol, derive a numeric worst-seed expected-net-bps threshold only from the complete policy-calibration role. A coverage passes only when the aggregate and both constituent UTC days pass every calibration gate. Select the passing coverage with the highest aggregate paired-stress mean, then lower drawdown, then lower coverage. Evaluation cannot repair the threshold.
economic_screen.trade_rule: A side must be replay-executable and every seed must predict positive after-cost expected payoff. Choose the side with the greater worst-seed expected payoff and abstain on ties. Do not add a profitable-probability threshold: the integrated expected payoff already weights probability and magnitude, and a 0.50 hit-rate rule incorrectly rejects positively skewed opportunities. Apply only the frozen numeric threshold learned before evaluation.

execution_target.entry_and_exit: Cross the contemporaneous ask to enter long and bid to exit long; reverse for short. Enforce observed quote age, side-specific L1 participation, latency, same-day exit, and one non-overlapping position per symbol.

architectures.source: Reuse and revalidate the sealed Round 52 direct-mean artifacts and predictions as the matched support-correct control.

architectures.target: For each side, factor exact executable after-cost payoff into a marginal magnitude distribution and a profitable-sign probability conditional on magnitude, then numerically integrate their joint distribution.

architectures.single_change: Append the sealed causal FinCast matrix to the identical executable CSM candidate.

csm_factorization.expected_payoff: For magnitude state k, compute P(M=k|X) * representative_M_k * (2 * P(Y>0|X,M=representative_M_k) - 1), sum over k, then multiply by the causal stop width in bps.
csm_factorization.magnitude_state_edges: Training-only quantiles [0.10, 0.30, 0.50, 0.70, 0.90] of abs(exact after-cost net bps) / causal stop width, producing six ordered states.
csm_factorization.magnitude_state_representatives: Arithmetic mean training magnitude in each state; no clipping, winsorization, outcome weighting, or evaluation refit.
csm_factorization.magnitude_target: abs(exact after-cost net bps) / causal stop width
csm_factorization.sign_conditioning: Train the sign head with causal X plus log1p(realized magnitude risk units). At inference, evaluate the sign head at every training-derived magnitude representative and integrate; actual future magnitude is never used to make a prediction.
csm_factorization.sign_target: 1 only when exact after-cost net bps is strictly positive
csm_factorization.tail_distribution: Construct the twelve-point signed joint distribution from six magnitudes times positive and non-positive sign probabilities; report q10, q90, CVaR10, and profitable probability without using them to tune the frozen action rule.

lightgbm.minimum_leaf_rows: max(64, min(512, ceil(0.002 * side-specific training rows)))

model_contract.probability_calibration: Fit one bounded temperature for each magnitude distribution and one bounded Platt map for each conditional-sign head on the dedicated probability-calibration role only. The sign calibration uses that role's realized magnitude solely to calibrate the conditional factor, never for action scoring.
model_contract.replay_executable_support: target valid AND selected side L1 participation <= 10% AND selected side target exit remains in the same UTC day. Quote-age eligibility is already enforced during feature construction.
model_contract.support_invariant: Training, early stopping, probability calibration, policy calibration, proper-score baselines, threshold derivation, evaluation, and replay must use the same side-specific replay-executable predicate.

objective: Test whether a genuine conditioning-sign-on-magnitude joint model repairs Round 52's unstable severity economics on exact executable payoffs, and whether sealed FinCast features add incremental proper-score and after-cost value over the matched deterministic CSM model.

severity_shrinkage_probe.result: All 36 gain/loss coefficients clipped at 1.0, leaving every variant identical to Round 52; calibration day 1 remained negative and day 2 contained only one positive trade.

ai: FinCast must improve mean joint log-loss skill and expected-payoff Spearman by at least 0.005 over the matched deterministic CSM candidate.
FinCast must improve both base and paired-stress policy economics without worse drawdown, concentration, trade count, or either calibration-day result.
AI cannot pass when the deterministic CSM candidate fails numerical, predictive, temporal, or economic gates.
economic: Thresholds and coverage are selected only on policy calibration; evaluation cannot choose or repair them.
The aggregate calibration interval and both constituent UTC days must each remain positive under base and paired stress with their required trade counts.

Evaluation is consumed diagnostic evidence and requires at least 30 non-overlapping closed trades, two positive symbols, bounded concentration, positive base and paired-stress return, and profit factor above one.
No leverage, ROI annualization, profitability statement, or live authority is permitted.
numerical: All source artifacts, support masks, role indexes, labels, magnitude edges, representatives, models, predictions, joint probabilities, and ledgers are finite and hash-bound.
Every side and role records total rows, executable rows, rejected rows, class support, and exact mask SHA-256.
Every magnitude distribution and signed joint distribution sums to one within 1e-6; every model reloads exactly; OpenCL records no CPU fallback.
Unsupported rows can never enter model fitting, calibration, threshold selection, scoring, or replay.
predictive: Positive evaluation joint-log-loss and magnitude ranked-probability skill versus training class prevalence for both sides of every symbol.
Positive expected-payoff MSE skill versus the executable training mean and minimum expected-payoff Spearman 0.03 for both sides of every symbol.
Minimum pairwise seed expected-payoff Spearman 0.50 and no nonfinite or negative magnitude representative.

research_basis.limitation: The accepted paper studies monthly U.S. equity excess returns, not intraday crypto or executable barrier payoffs.
research_basis.use: It supplies the exact factorization Round 53 tests: model magnitude marginally, condition sign on contemporaneous magnitude, and integrate the joint distribution for expected return.

research_basis.limitation: The theory is generic probabilistic classification and does not establish market edge.
research_basis.use: Expected-utility decisions require calibrated posterior probabilities, while grouping loss can remain after scalar recalibration; this supports replacing inert severity shrinkage with a new joint model.

research_basis.limitation: The paper evaluates conformal intervals rather than crypto trade payoffs.
research_basis.use: Non-stationary financial time series need conditional temporal calibration checks; aggregate metrics can conceal drift, motivating separate policy-calibration-day gates.

research_basis.limitation: The benchmark studies daily liquid U.S. equities and is a recent preprint.
research_basis.use: Pretrained time-series models can be useful priors, but gains over random walk are small and sparse; FinCast remains a strictly matched ablation rather than presumed alpha.

research_basis.limitation: The study uses hourly BTC and selected configurations; its results are not imported.
research_basis.use: Transaction costs can erase apparent directional predictability, so targets and decisions remain exact after-cost executable payoffs.

research_basis.limitation: The engine cannot reconstruct queue evidence absent from the source corpus.
research_basis.use: Passive-order claims require queue and latency evidence; Round 53 keeps conservative taker execution.
